title: Divide Whole Numbers by Fractions
why: To divide a whole number by a fraction (like "how many 1/2s in 3?") you lean on counting equal pieces and knowing how many unit fractions fill one whole. Warm those up and dividing by a fraction feels like simple counting.

diagnostic.q: In 1/3, the bottom number 3 tells you the whole is cut into how many equal parts?

learn.heading: How many unit fractions fill a whole?
learn.intro: A unit fraction is a fraction with 1 on top, like 1/2 or 1/3. The bottom number tells you how many of them fit in one whole. To count them across several wholes, multiply.
examples: Several wholes: halves in 3 wholes → each whole has 2 halves, so 3 × 2 = 6 halves.

tiers.intro: Count pieces one whole at a time.

items.q: A. How many 1/2 pieces are in 1 whole?
items.hint: The denominator 2 means 2 halves fill one whole.

items.q: B. How many 1/3 pieces are in 1 whole?
items.hint: The denominator 3 means 3 thirds fill one whole.

items.q: C. How many 1/4 pieces are in 1 whole?
items.hint: The denominator 4 means 4 fourths fill one whole.

tiers.intro: Now count across more than one whole.

items.q: A. How many halves (1/2) are in 2 wholes? (2 × 2)
items.hint: Each whole has 2 halves, so 2 wholes → 2 × 2 = 4.

items.q: B. How many thirds (1/3) are in 2 wholes?

tiers.intro: Stretch straight into dividing a whole by a fraction.

items.q: A. 3 ÷ 1/2 means "how many halves in 3 wholes?" What is it?
B. 2 ÷ 1/4 means "how many fourths in 2 wholes?" What is it?

exit.q: 1. How many fourths (1/4) are in 1 whole?
exit.hint: The denominator 4 means 4 fourths fill one whole.

exit.q: 2. 2 ÷ 1/2 means "how many halves in 2 wholes?" What is it?
